name: Millionaire's Fruitcake
recipeIngredient: 1 package (6oz) dried apricot halves; chopped (1 cup)
1/2 cup Diced candied lemon peel (4 oz)
recipeInstructions:
Day ahead or up to 1 month ahead:
1. Grease a 10-inch tube pan and line the bottom with foil.
2. In a large bowl, combine all fruit ingredients.
3. In another large bowl, beat sugar and shortening until blended.
4. Increase speed to high and beat until light and fluffy.
5. Add flour, baking powder, salt, vanilla extract, and eggs; mix on low until just combined.
6. Stir the batter into the fruit mixture.
7. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
8. Spoon batter into the prepared pan and bake for 1 1/2 hours or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
9. Cool the cake completely in the pan on a wire rack.
10. Remove from the pan and carefully peel off the foil.
11. Wrap the fruitcake tightly in plastic wrap or foil.
12. Refrigerate the fruitcake overnight to make it firm and easy to slice.
13. To serve, heat apricot preserves in a small saucepan over medium heat until just melted.
14. Brush cake with half of the apricot preserves.
15. Garnish the top of the cake with dried fruit, candied cherries, and pecans.
16. Brush remaining apricot preserves onto the fruit and pecans.
Makes one 4 3/4 pounds (2.2 kg) fruit cake or 36 servings. Each serving without garnish contains about 265 calories, 10g fat, 30mg cholesterol, and 90mg sodium.
